title: aciphylla flabellata
intro: Aciphylla flabellata, also known as the New Zealand astelia or wharawhara, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive, fan-shaped leaves and tall stalks of yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Aciphylla flabellata include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate rheumatism. Traditionally, the plant was prepared for medicinal use by making a tea from the leaves or roots, which were also used in poultices and other herbal preparations.
